title: Eva VS other robot shows
wikitext:
[]: Eva VS. other robot shows             Quote Post by GAP » Thu Jan 15, 2009 12:50 am I am sure if there are threads like this and I tried searching for it but nothing seem to come up. Eva as we know it is basically a deconstruction of giant robot shows with lots of mindrape to boost but how better is Eva compared to shows like Gundam or Macross? I had seen my fair share of robot shows (even Megas XLR) but Eva is something different entirely and compared to other robot shows this one is a sick, weird and almost nihilistic. How does Eva compare with other robots shows?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Jan 15, 2009 1:52 am TTGL pwns all. Even Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by jim_loves_randy » Thu Jan 15, 2009 2:44 am I heard only War in the Pocket comes close to Eva from Gundam in terms of quality and nihilism.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Thu Jan 15, 2009 9:39 am If Eva wasn't how it is, it would just me any other robot/mecha show. Probably really close to Gundam 00.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Jan 15, 2009 9:47 am Even if Eva was a standard mecha series, it would still be approximately 132,000 times better than anything named Gundam.

[]: Re: Eva VS. other robot shows             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Thu Jan 15, 2009 11:57 am GAP wrote: I am sure if there are threads like this and I tried searching for it

[]: Re: Eva VS. other robot shows             Quote Post by chee » Thu Jan 15, 2009 12:04 pm Dr. Nick wrote: Lots of other Gundam shows are nihilistic too, but 0080 is short and to the point, and arguably not as corny as them.

[]:             Quote Post by Skywalker007 » Fri Jan 16, 2009 4:28 pm There's nothing unique in those qualifiers, I would claim. The sickness level in many of Yoshiyuki Tomino's shows is on par with even the most shocking parts of EoE (Ideon and Zambot 3 come instantly to mind). Weirdness? Oh boy, as far as crazy plot elements or general nonsensicalness can go, there are plenty of weirder things out there than Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Fri Jan 16, 2009 4:43 pm Eva does for giant robot shows what Troilus and Cressida did for the Trojan War.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Jan 18, 2009 7:34 pm Id say Gurren Lagann is the best mecha I've seen since EVA.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Jan 19, 2009 8:07 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: Id say Gurren Lagann is the best mecha I've seen since EVA.

[]:             Quote Post by HENRCAST » Tue Jan 20, 2009 11:18 am BrikHaus wrote: Joseph the PRPD wrote: Id say Gurren Lagann is the best mecha I've seen since EVA.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Jan 20, 2009 11:46 am HENRCAST wrote: What does QFMFT mean?

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Tue Jan 20, 2009 12:48 pm I have not seen too many Mecha animes but NGE is my favorite I seen so far. I like Gundam when I was a kid but I stop watching the series since they made too many series like Gundam X and SD Gundam. I have not seen TTGL yet but I got it on DVD and I have plans to watch it soon.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Tue Jan 20, 2009 1:24 pm Eva

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jan 20, 2009 5:20 pm No more multi-quote cascades, if you please.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Jan 21, 2009 9:35 am I really wish Shinji would just once kick reason to the curb and go beyond the impossible.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jan 21, 2009 4:06 pm That would be in episode 19, I do believe.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jan 21, 2009 4:28 pm Don't forget about episode 04.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Jan 21, 2009 5:02 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Don't forget about episode 03 .

 16, 2009 4:28 pm There's nothing unique in those qualifiers, I would claim.
